title: State Police trooper accused of child abuse facing more charges
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Local News The unlawful wiretap and violation of abuse prevention order charges stem from incidents that occurred March 10.
Framingham State Police Headquarters.
Photo by John Tlumacki/The Boston Globe A State Police trooper accused of child abuse is facing more charges of unlawful wiretap and violating of abuse prevention order, prosecutors said.

title: Doc Rivers is reportedly out as Milwaukee Bucks coach
author: Khari A. Thompson

Rivers, 64, just wrapped up his third season with the Bucks.

title: Chelsea High School teacher on leave after students stage protest, allege misconduct
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Education “I've been told about her sex life with another teacher many times,” a student told the School Committee last week.
A Chelsea High School employee is on leave in the wake of an online petition and a protest at the high school accusing a teacher at the school of inappropriate conduct with students.
A staff member at Chelsea High School was placed on leave after “concerns were raised” recently about the teacher, Chelsea Public Schools said in a statement on Friday.

m.

title: Watch: Bill Belichick wins a challenge during Savannah Bananas coaching debut
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Sports News The Bananas made a trip to UNC and got the former Patriots coach to throw a yellow flag.

AP Photo/Karl DeBlaker The Savannah Bananas made a trip to Chapel Hill for an exhibition game on Saturday night, and they didn’t hesitate to make a certain former Patriots coach turned Tar Heel a part of the action.

title: Winthrop man charged with Social Security fraud, concealment
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Crime The man allegedly concealed a person’s death from the government so he could fraudulently receive their retirement benefits.
By Darin Zullo April 12, 2026 | 11:00 AM 1 minute to read A Winthrop man was charged Friday for allegedly committing Social Security fraud for over four years, according to prosecutors.
Kenneth Riel, 51, was indicted on one count of receiving stolen government money or property and one count of Social Security fraud-concealment, the U.
